article_meta.title: By Ellie CookSenior Defense ReporterShareNewsweek is a Trust Project memberU.S

reasoning: The model correctly identifies three genuine bias instances in the author's own prose: 'rambling speech' (subjective adjective), 'yawning chasm' (sensationalist language), and the 'unfathomable, until now' framing (opinion as fact). No quoted text is flagged. The bias type labels are appropriate and defensible. The model avoids flagging Trump's quoted statements or attributed source language. Minor quibble: the 'unfathomable, until now' could also be labeled as Spin or Word Choice, but Opinion Statements Presented as Fact is defensible. One could argue there's a minor miss on 'The White House had refused to rule out using military force to take Greenland, which would be a violation of international law' — the phrase 'which would be a violation of international law' could be seen as an opinion presented as fact (though many would consider it a factual legal assessment). Overall this is a strong, careful analysis.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All three flagged items are clearly from the author's own prose. No quoted or attributed text is flagged. Perfect compliance with the attribution rule.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: All three items are defensible genuine bias patterns in the author's own voice. 'Rambling speech' is clearly a subjective adjective. 'Yawning chasm' is sensationalist imagery. 'Unfathomable, until now' is an opinion presented as fact.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model catches the major instances. One could argue it missed 'which would be a violation of international law' as an opinion presented as fact (debatable — it's arguably a factual legal assessment but stated without attribution). The phrase 'refused to rule out' could be considered loaded framing (Spin). These are minor misses.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Labels are well-chosen. 'Rambling' as Subjective Qualifying Adjective is spot-on. 'Yawning chasm' as Sensationalism/Emotionalism is correct. 'Unfathomable, until now' as Opinion Statements Presented as Fact is defensible, though Spin could also work. Minor flexibility in labeling.
